_draft: POOL (accumulate phase; see QUIZBANK_RULES.md 7-A). All questions exam:wordpart, reconciled to medical_wordparts.json verbatim, bundle:null (unbundled). Bundling deferred to Phase B. NOT the final bank.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -itis?
questions.explain: -itis = inflammation. gastritis, arthritis, dermatitis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root cardi/o?
questions.explain: cardi/o = heart. cardiology, cardiomyopathy, bradycardia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -ectomy?
questions.explain: -ectomy = excision (surgical removal). appendectomy, nephrectomy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix hyper-?
questions.explain: hyper- = above or excessive. hypertension, hyperglycemia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix hypo-?
questions.explain: hypo- = below or deficient. hypothermia, hypoglycemia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -emia?
questions.explain: -emia = a blood condition. anemia, leukemia, hyperglycemia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -megaly?
questions.explain: -megaly = enlargement (from Greek megas, large). cardiomegaly, hepatomegaly.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -oma?
questions.explain: -oma = tumor or mass. carcinoma, lipoma, melanoma.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -algia?
questions.explain: -algia = pain. neuralgia, myalgia, arthralgia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix brady-?
questions.explain: brady- = slow. bradycardia (slow heart rate).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix tachy-?
questions.explain: tachy- = fast/rapid. tachycardia, tachypnea.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'heart'?
questions.explain: cardi/o = heart; hepat/o = liver; nephr/o = kidney; gastr/o = stomach.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'liver'?
questions.explain: hepat/o = liver. hepatitis, hepatomegaly.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'kidney'?
questions.explain: nephr/o = kidney. nephrology, nephrectomy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'stomach'?
questions.explain: gastr/o = stomach; enter/o = intestine; col/o = colon.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which suffix BEST corresponds to 'surgical creation of an opening'?
questions.explain: -ostomy = a new opening (colostomy); -otomy = incision; -ectomy = removal.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'joint'?
questions.explain: arthr/o = joint; oste/o = bone; my/o = muscle; chondr/o = cartilage.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'bone'?
questions.explain: oste/o = bone. osteoporosis, osteoarthritis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'nerve'?
questions.explain: neur/o = nerve. neuralgia, neuropathy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'gastritis'?
options: Removal of part of the stomach
questions.explain: -itis = inflammation. Every option keeps the stomach, so the suffix is what is tested: -ectomy (partial gastrectomy), -algia (gastralgia), -megaly (gastromegaly).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'cardiomegaly'?
questions.explain: cardi/o = heart. Every option is an enlargement, so -megaly is free and only the organ discriminates: hepatomegaly (liver), splenomegaly (spleen), nephromegaly (kidney).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'nephrectomy'?
questions.explain: nephr/o (kidney) + -ectomy (removal).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'hepatitis'?
questions.explain: hepat/o (liver) + -itis (inflammation).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'bradycardia'?
questions.explain: brady- (slow) + cardi/o (heart) + -ia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'tachycardia'?
questions.explain: tachy- (fast) + cardi/o (heart) + -ia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'arthritis'?
questions.explain: arthr/o (joint) + -itis (inflammation).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'neuralgia'?
questions.explain: neur/o (nerve) + -algia (pain).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'dermatitis'?
options: A tumor arising in the skin
questions.explain: dermat/o (skin) + -itis (inflammation).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'hepatomegaly'?
questions.explain: hepat/o (liver) + -megaly (enlargement). Splenomegaly is the same suffix applied to the spleen.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -osis?
questions.explain: -osis = abnormal condition or process. cyanosis, fibrosis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -pathy?
questions.explain: -pathy = disease. neuropathy, cardiomyopathy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -scopy?
questions.explain: -scopy = visual examination with a scope. colonoscopy, endoscopy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -plasty?
questions.explain: -plasty = surgical repair/reconstruction. rhinoplasty, arthroplasty.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix dys-?
questions.explain: dys- = difficult, painful, or abnormal. dysphagia, dyspnea.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix poly-?
questions.explain: poly- = many/much. polyuria, polyneuropathy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -penia?
questions.explain: -penia = deficiency. leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, osteopenia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -rrhea?
questions.explain: -rrhea = flow or discharge. diarrhea, rhinorrhea, otorrhea.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as nephr/o?
questions.explain: nephr/o (Greek) and ren/o (Latin) both = kidney. nephrology vs renal.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as rhin/o?
questions.explain: rhin/o (Greek, rhinoplasty) and nas/o (Latin, nasal) both = nose.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as ot/o?
questions.explain: ot/o (Greek, otitis) and aur/o (Latin, aural) both = ear.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as ophthalm/o?
questions.explain: ophthalm/o (Greek) and ocul/o (Latin, ocular) both = eye.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as gloss/o?
questions.explain: gloss/o (Greek, glossitis) and lingu/o (Latin, lingual) both = tongue.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which suffix shares the same meaning as -algia?
questions.explain: -algia and -dynia both = pain. neuralgia, pleurodynia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as dermat/o?
questions.explain: dermat/o (Greek, dermatology) and cutane/o (Latin, subcutaneous) both = skin.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix a- / an-?
questions.explain: a-/an- = without. anemia (without enough blood), apnea (without breathing).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix anti-?
questions.explain: anti- = against. antibiotic, antibody, antiseptic.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -ptosis?
questions.explain: -ptosis = drooping or prolapse. blepharoptosis, nephroptosis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -stenosis?
questions.explain: -stenosis = narrowing. aortic stenosis, pyloric stenosis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'muscle'?
questions.explain: my/o = muscle (myalgia); myel/o = spinal cord or bone marrow; myc/o = fungus; myring/o = eardrum.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'lung' or 'air'?
questions.explain: pneum/o = lung, air; bronch/o = bronchial tube; thorac/o = chest; laryng/o = voice box. Its Latin doublet pulmon/o is tested separately.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'blood'?
questions.explain: hemat/o = blood (hematology, hematoma); hydr/o = water; hepat/o = liver, a classic lookalike; cyt/o = cell.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root aden/o?
questions.explain: aden/o = gland. adenoma, adenitis, adenopathy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root acr/o?
questions.explain: acr/o = extremity or tip (also height). acromegaly, acrocyanosis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root chol/e?
questions.explain: chol/e = bile or gall. cholelithiasis, cholecystitis, cholestasis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -plegia?
questions.explain: -plegia = paralysis. quadriplegia, hemiplegia, ophthalmoplegia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -pnea?
questions.explain: -pnea = breathing. apnea, dyspnea, orthopnea.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -lysis?
questions.explain: -lysis = breakdown or destruction. hemolysis, thrombolysis, fibrinolysis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -centesis?
questions.explain: -centesis = surgical puncture to withdraw fluid. amniocentesis, thoracentesis, paracentesis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -tripsy?
questions.explain: -tripsy = crushing or breaking up. lithotripsy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'skull'?
questions.explain: crani/o = skull; cost/o = rib; cerebr/o = brain; chondr/o = cartilage.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'urinary bladder'?
questions.explain: cyst/o = bladder; pyel/o = renal pelvis; nephr/o = kidney; ureter/o = ureter.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'uterus'?
questions.explain: hyster/o = uterus; oophor/o and ovari/o = ovary; colp/o = vagina.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'fallopian tube'?
questions.explain: salping/o = fallopian tube; oophor/o = ovary; metr/o = uterus; colp/o = vagina.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'renal pelvis'?
questions.explain: pyel/o = renal pelvis; cyst/o = bladder; nephr/o = kidney; ureter/o = ureter.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'adenoma'?
questions.explain: aden/o (gland) + -oma (tumor); a benign glandular tumor.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'cholecystitis'?
options: Impaired or halted bile flow from the liver
questions.explain: chol/e = bile; cyst = sac, so cholecystitis is the gallbladder. Three options describe the ducts or bile flow - cholangitis, choledocholithiasis, cholestasis - so the majority pattern is a deliberate trap.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'nephropexy'?
options: Surgical fixation of a displaced kidney
Surgical removal of a kidney damaged by disease
questions.explain: nephr/o (kidney) + -pexy (surgical fixation).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'thoracentesis'?
options: Needle puncture of the chest to drain fluid
questions.explain: thorac/o (chest) + -centesis (puncture to withdraw fluid).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as adip/o?
questions.explain: adip/o (Latin) and lip/o (Greek) both = fat. adipose vs liposuction.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as mast/o?
questions.explain: mast/o (Greek, mastectomy) and mamm/o (Latin, mammogram) both = breast.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as hyster/o?
questions.explain: hyster/o and metr/o both = uterus. hysterectomy vs endometrium.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as dactyl/o?
questions.explain: dactyl/o (Greek) and digit/o (Latin) both = finger or toe. polydactyly vs digital.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -logy?
questions.explain: -logy = study of. cardiology, pathology, immunology.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -gram?
questions.explain: -gram = the record or image produced. electrocardiogram, mammogram; -graphy is the process.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -therapy?
questions.explain: -therapy = treatment. chemotherapy, immunotherapy, radiotherapy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -uria?
questions.explain: -uria = a urine condition. hematuria, glycosuria, bacteriuria.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -emesis?
questions.explain: -emesis = vomiting. hematemesis, hyperemesis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -stasis?
questions.explain: -stasis = stopping or controlling. hemostasis, cholestasis, homeostasis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -plasia?
questions.explain: -plasia = formation, development, growth. hyperplasia, dysplasia, aplasia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix erythro-?
questions.explain: erythro- = red. erythrocyte (red blood cell), erythema.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix leuko-?
questions.explain: leuko- = white. leukocyte (white blood cell), leukemia, leukopenia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -spasm?
questions.explain: -spasm = a sudden involuntary contraction. bronchospasm, vasospasm.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'voice box'?
questions.explain: laryng/o = larynx (voice box); pharyng/o = throat; trache/o = windpipe; bronch/o = bronchial tube.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the throat passage that carries both food and air'?
questions.explain: pharyng/o = pharynx (throat); laryng/o = voice box; esophag/o = esophagus.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'windpipe'?
questions.explain: trache/o = trachea (windpipe); bronch/o = bronchial tube; laryng/o = voice box.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'chest'?
questions.explain: thorac/o = chest; cost/o = rib; stern/o = breastbone; abdomin/o = abdomen.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'spinal cord or bone marrow'?
questions.explain: myel/o = spinal cord or bone marrow; my/o = muscle; oste/o = bone; mening/o = meninges.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'craniotomy'?
options: Surgical repair of a skull defect
questions.explain: crani/o (skull) + -tomy (incision, cutting). Compare cranioplasty, the surgical repair of a skull defect.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'thrombocytopenia'?
options: A clot that broke free and blocked a vessel
A tendency to form abnormal clots
questions.explain: thromb/o (clot) + cyt/o (cell) + -penia (deficiency); a low platelet count.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'encephalitis'?
options: Fluid accumulation in the spinal cord
questions.explain: encephal/o (brain) + -itis (inflammation). The options pair inflammation/fluid accumulation with brain/spinal cord, so both parts must be known: myelitis, hydrocephalus, syringomyelia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'lymphadenopathy'?
options: A white blood cell of the immune system
questions.explain: lymph/o (lymph) + aden/o (gland or node) + -pathy (disease); enlarged lymph nodes.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'hydrocephalus'?
options: Accumulation of fluid within the skull
questions.explain: hydr/o (water, fluid) + cephal/o (head); fluid accumulation within the skull.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'colonoscopy'?
options: A surgical opening from the colon
questions.explain: colon/o (colon) + -scopy (visual examination).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'angioplasty'?
options: Surgical repair of a blocked blood vessel
A benign tumor of blood vessels
questions.explain: angi/o (blood vessel) + -plasty (surgical repair).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'necrosis'?
questions.explain: necr/o (death, dead tissue) + -osis (condition); death of body tissue.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'immunosuppression'?
questions.explain: immun/o (immune) + suppression; lowering immune activity, as after a transplant.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as encephal/o?
questions.explain: encephal/o (Greek) and cerebr/o (Latin) both = brain. encephalitis vs cerebral.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as angi/o?
questions.explain: angi/o (Greek) and vascul/o (Latin) both = blood vessel. angiogram vs vascular.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as endo-?
questions.explain: endo- (Greek) and intra- (Latin) both = within, inside. endoscopy vs intravenous.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as muscul/o?
questions.explain: muscul/o (Latin) and my/o (Greek) both = muscle. musculoskeletal vs myalgia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as ren/o?
questions.explain: ren/o (Latin) and nephr/o (Greek) both = kidney. renal failure vs nephrology.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as pre-?
questions.explain: pre- and ante- both = before. prenatal vs antepartum.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -graphy?
questions.explain: -graphy = the process of recording or imaging (mammography); -gram is the record produced (mammogram).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -sclerosis?
questions.explain: -sclerosis = hardening. atherosclerosis, multiple sclerosis. Compare -stenosis (narrowing).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -rrhage / -rrhagia?
questions.explain: -rrhage = excessive flow (hemorrhage). Compare -rrhea (flow/discharge), -rrhexis (rupture), -rrhaphy (suturing).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -opsy?
questions.explain: -opsy = examination of tissue. biopsy, autopsy. Compare -scopy (viewing with a scope).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -cyte?
questions.explain: -cyte = cell. leukocyte (white cell), erythrocyte (red cell), thrombocyte (platelet).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix meta-?
questions.explain: meta- = change, beyond, after. metastasis, metabolism, metaplasia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix mal-?
questions.explain: mal- = bad, abnormal. malnutrition, malformation, malabsorption.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix auto-?
questions.explain: auto- = self. autoimmune, autograft, autologous.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root ather/o?
options: Fatty plaque in an artery wall
A blood clot lodged in an artery
Fat tissue stored beneath the skin
questions.explain: ather/o = fatty plaque. atheroma, atherosclerosis. Compare lip/o (fat), thromb/o (clot), sclero- (hardening).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -asthenia?
questions.explain: -asthenia = weakness. myasthenia gravis. Compare -plegia (paralysis), -paresis (partial paralysis).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'vein'?
questions.explain: phleb/o = vein (phlebotomy); arteri/o = artery; angi/o = blood vessel generally; lymph/o = lymph.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'eardrum'?
questions.explain: tympan/o = eardrum; ot/o and aur/o = ear; audi/o = hearing.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'hearing'?
questions.explain: audi/o = hearing (audiometry); ot/o and aur/o = the ear itself; tympan/o = eardrum.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'tooth'?
questions.explain: odont/o = tooth (orthodontics); gloss/o and lingu/o = tongue; pharyng/o = throat.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the membrane around the fetus'?
questions.explain: amni/o = amnion; salping/o = fallopian tube; hyster/o and metr/o = uterus.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'stiff or fused'?
questions.explain: ankyl/o = stiff, bent, fused (ankylosis); arthr/o = joint; oste/o = bone; chondr/o = cartilage.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'upper chamber of the heart'?
questions.explain: atri/o = atrium (upper chamber); ventricul/o = ventricle (lower chamber); sept/o = the partition between them.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'partition'?
questions.explain: sept/o = septum, partition (septoplasty). Beware: sepsis/septic come from a different Greek root meaning rot, not sept/o.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'atherosclerosis'?
options: Hardening of arteries from fatty plaque buildup
A fatty plaque deposit in an artery wall
Surgical removal of plaque from a vessel to restore blood flow
Formation of fatty plaque in artery walls
questions.explain: ather/o (fatty plaque) + -sclerosis (hardening). Compare atheroma (the plaque itself), atherectomy (its removal).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'analgesic'?
options: An instrument that measures pain sensitivity
questions.explain: an- (without) + alges/o (pain sensitivity) + -ic; a pain-relieving drug. Compare analgesia (the absence of pain itself).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'amniocentesis'?
options: Withdrawal of amniotic fluid for testing
Visual examination of the amniotic sac
questions.explain: amni/o (amnion) + -centesis (surgical puncture to withdraw fluid).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'ankylosis'?
options: Stiffening and immobility of a joint
Inflammatory disease fusing the spinal vertebrae
Fusion of adjacent fingers or toes
questions.explain: ankyl/o (stiff, fused) + -osis (condition); a stiff, immobile joint.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'menarche'?
questions.explain: men/o (menstruation) + -arche (beginning, onset). Amenorrhea is its absence and dysmenorrhea is painful menstruation - only -arche carries the sense of onset.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'audiometry'?
questions.explain: audi/o (hearing) + -metry (measurement). Compare audiology (the study), audiogram (the graph).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'autologous'?
options: Derived from the person's own body
Immune attack on one's own tissue
Tissue from a donor of another species
questions.explain: auto- (self); autologous blood or tissue comes from the same individual. Compare autoimmune, autopsy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'acidosis'?
options: An agent that neutralizes stomach acid
questions.explain: acid/o (acid) + -osis (abnormal condition).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'agglutination'?
options: Clumping of cells, as in blood typing
Clumping of red blood cells specifically
An antigen that stimulates clumping antibodies
questions.explain: agglutin/o = clumping, sticking together. Compare agglutinin (the antibody), agglutinogen (the antigen).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'astrocytoma'?
options: A tumor arising from star-shaped nerve support cells
A star-shaped supporting cell of the nervous system
questions.explain: astr/o (star-shaped) + cyt/o (cell) + -oma (tumor); a tumor of astrocytes.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as odont/o?
questions.explain: odont/o (Greek) and dent/i (Latin) both = tooth. orthodontics vs dentist.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as enter/o?
questions.explain: enter/o (Greek) and intestin/o (Latin) both = intestine. enteritis vs intestinal.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as tympan/o?
questions.explain: tympan/o and myring/o both = eardrum. tympanoplasty vs myringotomy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as vesic/o?
questions.explain: vesic/o (Latin) and cyst/o (Greek) both = bladder. vesicoureteral reflux vs cystoscopy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as mega-?
questions.explain: mega- and macro- both = large. megaloblast vs macrocyte. micro- is the opposite.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as mono-?
questions.explain: mono- (Greek) and uni- (Latin) both = one, single. monocyte vs unilateral.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as contra-?
questions.explain: contra- (Latin) and anti- (Greek) both = against. contraindication vs antibiotic.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as phleb/o?
questions.explain: phleb/o (Greek) and ven/o (Latin) both = vein. phlebotomy vs venous.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as ambi-?
questions.explain: ambi- (Latin) and amphi- (Greek) both = both, around. ambidextrous vs amphiarthrosis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -edema?
questions.explain: -edema = swelling, fluid accumulation. lymphedema, papilledema, angioedema.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -eurysm?
options: Widening or dilation of a vessel wall
Narrowing of a passage or opening
questions.explain: -eurysm = widening, dilation of a vessel wall. aneurysm. Compare -stenosis (narrowing), -sclerosis (hardening).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -phagia?
questions.explain: -phagia = swallowing, eating. dysphagia = difficulty swallowing; polyphagia = excessive eating. Do not confuse with phas/o (speech), as in aphasia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root phas/o?
questions.explain: phas/o = speech, language. aphasia = loss of language, as after a stroke. Do not confuse with -phagia (swallowing), as in dysphagia. The older term dysphasia has largely been abandoned in clinical use precisely because it was so often mistaken for dysphagia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -tension?
questions.explain: -tension = pressure. hypertension, hypotension. It is from Latin tendere (to stretch), not ten/o (tendon).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root phylax/o?
questions.explain: phylax/o = guard, protect. prophylaxis, anaphylaxis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'tumor'?
questions.explain: onc/o = tumor (oncology); path/o = disease; cyt/o = cell; crin/o = secrete.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'cornea or horny tissue'?
questions.explain: kerat/o = cornea, hard horny tissue; ocul/o = eye; retin/o = retina; blephar/o = eyelid.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the membranes covering the brain and spinal cord'?
questions.explain: mening/o = the membranes covering the brain and spinal cord; encephal/o = brain; myel/o = spinal cord; crani/o = skull.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'neck'?
questions.explain: cervic/o = neck, or the cervix of the uterus; thorac/o = chest; lumb/o = lower back; crani/o = skull.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'ureter'?
questions.explain: ureter/o = the tube from kidney to bladder; urethr/o = the tube from bladder to outside; ur/o = urine; vesic/o = bladder.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'urethra'?
questions.explain: urethr/o = the tube carrying urine out of the body; ureter/o = kidney to bladder; ur/o = urine; cyst/o = bladder.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'flesh or connective tissue'?
questions.explain: sarc/o = flesh, connective tissue (sarcoma); lip/o = fat; chondr/o = cartilage; oste/o = bone.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'side'?
questions.explain: later/o = side (bilateral); medi/o = middle; thorac/o = chest; abdomin/o = abdomen.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'carcinogen'?
options: A substance capable of causing cancer
A malignant tumor of epithelial tissue
The process by which cells become cancerous
questions.explain: carcin/o (cancer) + -gen (producing); a cancer-causing agent. Compare carcinoma (the tumor), carcinogenesis (the process).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'anticoagulant'?
options: An agent that inhibits blood clot formation
The process of blood clot formation
A disorder of the clotting mechanism
questions.explain: anti- (against) + coagul/o (clotting); a drug that prevents clots. Compare coagulopathy (a clotting disorder).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'meningocele'?
options: Protrusion of the meninges through a bony defect
A benign tumor of the meninges
questions.explain: -cele = hernia, protrusion. Every option keeps the meninges, so the suffix is tested: meningitis (-itis), meningioma (-oma), meningococcemia (-emia).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'orthopnea'?
options: Pertaining to an upright standing position
questions.explain: orth/o (straight, upright) + -pnea (breathing); breathlessness lying flat, relieved by sitting up.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'prophylaxis'?
options: Preventive treatment to guard against disease
A rapidly diminishing response to repeated drug doses
The study of drug action in the body
questions.explain: pro- (before) + phylax/o (guard, protect); preventive treatment. Compare anaphylaxis, tachyphylaxis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'electroencephalogram'?
options: A recording of the brain's electrical activity
A recording of the heart's electrical activity
A recording of muscle electrical activity
A mineral ion that conducts electrical impulses
questions.explain: electr/o (electricity) + encephal/o (brain) + -gram (record). Compare electrocardiogram (heart), electromyogram (muscle).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'toxemia'?
options: Presence of toxins in the bloodstream
The study of poisons and their effects
Extreme dilation of the colon complicating colitis
A parasitic infection dangerous in pregnancy
questions.explain: tox/i (poison) + -emia (blood condition); toxins in the blood.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'psychosis'?
options: A severe mental disorder with loss of contact with reality
Treatment of mental disorders by psychological means
Use of medication to treat psychiatric disorders
questions.explain: psych/o (mind) + -osis (abnormal condition). Compare psychiatry, psychotherapy, psychopharmacology.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'bacteremia'?
options: Presence of bacteria in the bloodstream
Presence of bacteria in the urine
An agent that inhibits bacterial growth
questions.explain: bacter/i (bacteria) + -emia (blood condition). Compare bacteriuria (bacteria in urine).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'mucolytic'?
options: An agent that thins or dissolves mucus
The moist membrane lining body cavities
questions.explain: muc/o (mucus) + -lytic (breaking down); a drug that loosens mucus. Compare mucosa, mucositis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'keratitis'?
questions.explain: kerat/o = cornea. Every option is an inflammation, so -itis is free and only the structure discriminates: blepharitis (eyelid), iritis (iris), conjunctivitis (conjunctiva).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as nas/o?
questions.explain: nas/o (Latin) and rhin/o (Greek) both = nose. nasal vs rhinoplasty.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as aur/o?
questions.explain: aur/o (Latin) and ot/o (Greek) both = ear. aural vs otitis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which suffix shares the same meaning as -dynia?
questions.explain: -dynia and -algia both = pain. pleurodynia vs neuralgia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix xantho-?
questions.explain: xantho- = yellow. xanthoma (yellow skin deposit), xanthelasma, xanthochromia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix porphyr-?
questions.explain: porphyr- = purple. porphyria (purple-pigment disorder), porphyrinuria.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the prefix polio-?
questions.explain: polio-/glauco- = gray. poliomyelitis (gray matter of the spinal cord), glaucoma.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the root hidr/o?
questions.explain: hidr/o = sweat. hyperhidrosis, anhidrosis. Do not confuse with hydr/o = water.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST describes the suffix -clasia?
questions.explain: -clasia/-clasis = breaking. osteoclasis, arthroclasia. Contrast -tripsy (crushing), -desis (fusion), -pexy (fixation).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the smallest venous vessels, which collect blood from the capillary beds'?
questions.explain: venul/o = venule (a small vein). Contrast arteriol/o (small artery), ven/o (vein), ventricul/o (ventricle). venulitis, postcapillary venule.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the middle segment of the brainstem, between the midbrain and the medulla'?
questions.explain: pont/o = pons (part of the brainstem). Contrast encephal/o (brain), crani/o (skull), neur/o (nerve). pontine, pontocerebellar.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the glands and ducts that produce and drain tears'?
questions.explain: dacry/o = tear, tear duct. Contrast ocul/o (eye), blephar/o (eyelid), kerat/o (cornea). dacryocystitis, dacryoadenitis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the stirrup-shaped bone of the middle ear, the smallest bone in the body'?
questions.explain: staped/o = stapes (stirrup bone). Contrast tympan/o and myring/o (eardrum), ot/o (ear). stapedectomy, stapedial.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the dome-shaped muscle that separates the chest cavity from the abdomen'?
questions.explain: phren/o = diaphragm (also 'mind'). Trap: diaphor/o = sweating, NOT diaphragm. Contrast thorac/o (chest), pneum/o (lung/air). phrenic nerve.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the internal organs of the abdominal cavity, considered collectively'?
questions.explain: splanchn/o = viscera, internal organs. Contrast gastr/o (stomach), enter/o (intestine), hepat/o (liver). splanchnic, splanchnology.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the brain's central relay station that routes sensory signals to the cortex'?
questions.explain: thalam/o = thalamus. Contrast encephal/o (brain), neur/o (nerve), mening/o (meninges). thalamic, thalamotomy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the last section of the bowel, ending at the anal canal'?
questions.explain: proct/o = rectum, anus. Contrast colon/o (colon), enter/o (intestine), gastr/o (stomach). proctoscopy, proctitis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the gland in the upper chest where T lymphocytes mature'?
questions.explain: thym/o = thymus gland. Do not confuse with thyr/o (thyroid). Contrast lymph/o, aden/o (gland). thymoma, thymectomy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root BEST corresponds to 'the outer membrane layer that lines the wall of a body cavity'?
questions.explain: pariet/o = cavity wall (parietal). Opposite of viscer/o (the organ-covering, visceral layer). Contrast pleur/o, thorac/o. parietal pleura, parietal peritoneum.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Arteriolosclerosis'?
options: Hardening of the walls of the smallest arterial branches
Hardening of the walls of the large elastic arteries
Hardening of the walls of the small veins
Narrowing of an arteriole by a blood clot
questions.explain: arteriol/o = arteriole (a small artery). Arteriolosclerosis is hardening of the arterioles, distinct from arteriosclerosis (large arteries). arteriolitis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Oocyte'?
questions.explain: oo/o = egg, ovum. An oocyte is an immature egg cell; contrast spermatocyte, osteocyte, myocyte. oogenesis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Azotemia'?
options: Buildup of nitrogen-containing waste in the blood
Buildup of excess acid in the blood
Deficiency of oxygen in the blood
questions.explain: azot/o = nitrogen, nitrogenous waste. Azotemia is nitrogen waste in the blood; contrast acidemia, hyperglycemia, hypoxemia. hyperazotemia.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Diaphoresis'?
questions.explain: diaphor/o = profuse sweating. Diaphoresis is heavy sweating, as in shock or fever; contrast adiaphoresis (absence of sweating).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Coccydynia'?
questions.explain: coccyg/o = coccyx (tailbone); -dynia = pain. Coccydynia is tailbone pain. coccygectomy, sacrococcygeal.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Sphygmomanometer'?
options: An instrument for measuring blood pressure
An instrument for measuring hearing acuity
An instrument for measuring body temperature
An instrument for recording the heart's electrical activity
questions.explain: sphygm/o = pulse. A sphygmomanometer measures blood pressure from the arterial pulse. sphygmograph.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Balanitis'?
questions.explain: balan/o = glans penis. Balanitis is inflammation of the glans; contrast posthitis (foreskin), orchitis (testis). balanoposthitis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Orchiectomy'?
questions.explain: orchi/o = testis. Every option is a removal, so -ectomy is free and only the organ discriminates: prostatectomy, epididymectomy, circumcision.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Ichthyosis'?
options: Dry, rough, scaly skin resembling fish scales
Oily, greasy, yellow scaling of the skin
Tense, fluid-filled blisters on the skin
Raised, itchy red welts on the skin
questions.explain: ichthy/o = dry, scaly, fish-like. Ichthyosis is fish-scale skin; contrast seborrhea (oily). ichthyosis vulgaris.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Rhytidectomy'?
options: Surgical removal of excess eyelid skin
Surgical removal of a skin tumor
questions.explain: rhytid/o = wrinkle. Rhytidectomy (facelift) removes skin wrinkles; contrast blepharoplasty (eyelid). rhytidoplasty.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Xerostomia'?
options: Excessive saliva production in the mouth
questions.explain: xer/o = dry. Xerostomia is dry mouth from reduced saliva. xerosis (dry skin), xerophthalmia (dry eyes).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Sialadenitis'?
questions.explain: sialaden/o = salivary gland. Sialadenitis is salivary-gland inflammation; contrast dacryoadenitis (tear gland), lymphadenitis (lymph node). sialadenosis.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Varicocele'?
options: Enlargement of the veins within the scrotum
Enlargement of the veins of the leg
A fluid-filled swelling within the scrotum
A fluid-filled swelling in the leg
questions.explain: varic/o = swollen, varicose vein; -cele = swelling. The options pair veins/fluid with scrotum/leg: varicose veins, hydrocele, leg edema.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Exsanguination'?
questions.explain: sanguin/o = blood. Exsanguination is massive blood loss. consanguinity (blood relation), sanguineous.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which option BEST defines 'Pinealoma'?
options: A tumor of the small brain gland that secretes melatonin and governs sleep
A tumor of the gland at the base of the brain that directs other endocrine glands
A tumor of the chest gland where T lymphocytes mature
A tumor of a hormone gland sitting atop the kidney
questions.explain: pineal/o = pineal gland (the melatonin-secreting, sleep-regulating gland deep in the brain). A pinealoma is a pineal tumor; contrast pituitary adenoma, thymoma. pinealectomy.

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as vascul/o?
questions.explain: vascul/o and angi/o both mean vessel (Latin and Greek). Seen in vascular, angiography, angioplasty; contrast arteri/o (artery), ven/o (vein), lymph/o (lymph).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as intestin/o?
questions.explain: intestin/o and enter/o both mean intestine (Latin and Greek). Seen in intestinal, enteritis, gastroenterology; contrast gastr/o (stomach), colon/o (colon), proct/o (rectum).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as metr/o?
questions.explain: metr/o and hyster/o both mean uterus. Seen in endometrium, hysterectomy; contrast ovari/o (ovary), salping/o (fallopian tube), cervic/o (cervix).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as dent/i?
questions.explain: dent/i and odont/o both mean tooth (Latin and Greek). Seen in dentist, orthodontics, periodontal; contrast gloss/o (tongue), bucc/o (cheek), labi/o (lip).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as myring/o?
questions.explain: myring/o and tympan/o both mean eardrum. Seen in myringotomy, tympanoplasty; contrast ot/o (ear), staped/o (stapes), acous/o (hearing).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as cyst/o?
questions.explain: cyst/o and vesic/o both mean bladder or sac. Seen in cystoscopy, vesicoureteral reflux; contrast ureter/o (ureter), urethr/o (urethra), pyel/o (renal pelvis).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as digit/o?
questions.explain: digit/o and dactyl/o both mean finger or toe (Latin and Greek). Seen in digital, polydactyly, syndactyly; contrast carp/o (wrist), brachi/o (arm), crur/o (leg).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as cutane/o?
questions.explain: cutane/o and dermat/o both mean skin (Latin and Greek). Seen in subcutaneous, dermatology; contrast onych/o (nail), trich/o (hair), seb/o (sebum).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as cerebr/o?
questions.explain: cerebr/o and encephal/o both mean brain (Latin and Greek). Seen in cerebral, encephalitis; contrast mening/o (meninges), crani/o (skull), myel/o (spinal cord).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which prefix shares the same meaning as intra-?
questions.explain: intra- and endo- both mean within or inside. Seen in intravenous, endoscopy; contrast exo- (outside), peri- (around), epi- (upon).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as lingu/o?
questions.explain: lingu/o and gloss/o both mean tongue (Latin and Greek). Seen in sublingual, glossitis; contrast dent/i (tooth), bucc/o (cheek), pharyng/o (throat).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as mamm/o?
questions.explain: mamm/o and mast/o both mean breast (Latin and Greek). Seen in mammogram, mastectomy; contrast lact/o (milk), thorac/o (chest), axill/o (armpit).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as ocul/o?
questions.explain: ocul/o and ophthalm/o both mean eye (Latin and Greek). Seen in ocular, ophthalmology; contrast blephar/o (eyelid), retin/o (retina), dacry/o (tear duct).

questions.prompt: In medical terminology, which root shares the same meaning as ven/o?
questions.explain: ven/o and phleb/o both mean vein (Latin and Greek). Seen in venous, phlebotomy; contrast arteri/o (artery), angi/o (vessel), arteriol/o (arteriole).
